title: Rhinanthus elatior
taxonomy: Rhinanthus elatior is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae. This family is commonly known as the broomrapes or milkworts. Rhinanthus elatior is further classified as a member of the genus Rhinanthus. The genus is comprised of various species of flowering plants, commonly known as the pink purslanes. Rhinanthus elatior is often referred to as the greater broomrape due to its distinctive broom-like appearance.

intro: Rhinanthus elatior, commonly known as the greater wormwood, is a perennial flowering plant that belongs to the family Orobanchaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever reduction and gastrointestinal problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Rhinanthus elatior contains bioactive constituents such as iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Rhinanthus elatior include teas, tinctures, and infusions, often used to support digestion and relieve symptoms of colds and flu.
